mission.title: Install and Configure MongoDB Community Operator
mission.description: The MongoDB Community Operator allows you to deploy and manage MongoDB Community instances on Kubernetes. It simplifies the deployment process and provides lifecycle management for MongoDB clusters. However, it is deprecated, and users are encouraged to migrate to the new MongoDB Kubernetes Operator for continued support and updates.

steps.title: Add the MongoDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the MongoDB Community Operator Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add mongodb https://mongodb.github.io/mongodb-kubernetes-operator

helm repo update

```

> This adds the official MongoDB Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the MongoDB Community Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the MongoDB Community Operator in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace mongodb

helm install mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

```

> Creates a namespace for MongoDB resources.
> Installs the MongoDB Community Operator.

steps.title: Verify the Operator installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the MongoDB Community Operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n mongodb

```

> Lists all pods in the mongodb namespace to verify the operator is running.

steps.title: Create a MongoDB custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a MongoDB custom resource to deploy a MongoDB instance.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.3
  podSpec:
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: "100m"
        memory: "512Mi"
EOF

```

> Creates a MongoDB replica set with 3 members.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.3
  podSpec:
    resources:
      requests:
        cpu: "100m"
        memory: "512Mi"
EOF

steps.title: Verify the MongoDB instance
steps.description:
This step checks if the MongoDB instance is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb

```

> Lists all MongoDB instances in the mongodb namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the MongoDB Community Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the MongoDB Community Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall mongodb-community-operator --namespace mongodb

kubectl delete namespace mongodb

```

> Uninstalls the MongoDB Community Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the MongoDB Community Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have backups of your MongoDB data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n 1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

kubectl uncordon <pod-name>

```

> Drains the MongoDB pods.
> Upgrades the MongoDB Community Operator.
> Uncordons the MongoDB pods after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n 1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create MongoDB resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB custom resource definition (CRD) may not be installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator/master/deploy/crds/mongodb.com_mongodb_crd.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB instance may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource requests in the MongoDB custom resource definition.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused' when accessing MongoDB
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration and ensure it is of type 'ClusterIP' or 'LoadBalancer'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found' when checking MongoDB instance
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB custom resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Verify the creation command and check for errors in the operator logs.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for MongoDB version 5.0.3, improved logging capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.11, ensure to migrate your MongoDB resources to the new repository.

resolution.summary: You should see the MongoDB instance listed with 'my-mongo' in the output of 'kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
